title: streptanthus bernardinus
intro: Streptanthus bernardinus, also known as the Santa Barbara jewelflower, is a rare and endangered wildflower native to the coastal regions of California. It belongs to the family Brassicaceae and is characterized by its bright yellow and orange flowers with distinctive, linear, and pointed petals. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, which have been attributed to its high content of glucosinolates and other bioactive compounds. Herbal preparations of Streptanthus bernardinus are typically made from the plant's leaves and flowers and can be used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and skin irritations.
